You can get 15% off of floating sound baths in Edinburgh to celebrate. We have space this Saturday & Sunday.

Enter code вАЬSUMMERвАЭ at checkout.

The main themes for the summer solstice are:

* Gratitude for what has grown or come into fruition
* Celebrating vitality, creativity & expansion
* Connecting with nature & seasonal cycles
* Setting intentions for the second half of the year

This is one of my favourite phases in the year as the sun is such a powerful force. A floating sound bath offers a way to experience that energy in a deeply embodied way. 

Water creates a weightless, supported state where the nervous system can fully downshift, while sound frequencies help release tension and quiet mental noise.

Feedback like this is exactly why we do what we do.

A full-day, nine-hour hybrid meeting for over 40 participants is no small undertaking, the kind of day where small things can easily go wrong if a venue isn't properly set up for it. Comfortable room, the right tech, catering that keeps everyone going from morning through to the afternoon.

But what stood out most to Charlie wasn't just the room itself. It was having a team genuinely available throughout the day, ready to help with whatever came up, whenever it came up. That's the part of the job that doesn't always make it onto a venue's spec sheet, but it's often the part that matters most once the day is actually underway.

Thank you for taking the time to share this. It means a great deal to the whole team.

If we can, we will.

Spotlight onвА¶
Man of Moon вЬ®
Haddstock Headliner Gig Sat 30th May 

A standout live act since their beginning, this Glasgow-based duo possess an extraordinary talent for cross-pollinating genres and styles but their sound is not hybridised, rather they are a synthesis of their influences; a pure sonic distillation of what has come before. You hear the mesmeric drones of Mogwai, the dynamic shifts of Radiohead, the harmonic angularity of Tool, the industrial amalgam of Nine Inch Nails, even hints of Pink FloydвАЩs austere and abstract lyricism. Man of Moon are so much music in one band. And yet theyвАЩve always sounded uniquely themselves; there is deep intentionality in every songвАЩs construction.

вАЬOne of the best bands in the countryвАЭ [Snack Magazine]

вАЬThe best debut single by a UK band since Ceremony by New OrderвАЭ [The New York Times]

вАЬOnes to watchвАЭ [NME]

вАЬ4/5 stars, noirish transportational krautrockвАЭ [The Guardian]

вАЬpushing the 2-peice prototype as far as it can goвАЭ [Vic Galloway, BBC Scotland)]

We can't wait to host Man of Moon at Haddington Corn Exchange in partnership with The Brunton on Sat 30th May as part of Haddstock. You can buy tickets on The Brunton website - see FB event page or Insta bio for link.
